data.title: RTX wins $380M navy contract; Lockheed gets $202M army award
data.description:
• Raytheon Co. of Tucson, Arizona, a business unit of RTX Corp. (RTX), was awarded a $380.8 million U.S. Navy contract modification, the Defense Department said on Wednesday.
  • Lockheed Martin (LMT) was awarded a $202.8 million contract modification to inspect, recertify, and repair PAC-3 missiles, returning them to serviceable condition, the statement read. The work will be performed in Grand Prairie and is expected to be completed by June 30, 2028.

data.title: Japan’s trade surplus tightens to JPY 105.7B as government stimulus sparks import growth surge
data.description:
Japan’s trade surplus declined to JPY 105.7 billion in December 2025, down from JPY 120.3 billion a year earlier, missing expectations for a JPY 357 billion increase as imports grew faster than exports.

Exports rose by 5.1% year-on-year to JPY 10,411.5 billion, hitting a record high but falling short of November’s growth rate and market forecasts due to ongoing U.S. tariffs on Japanese automakers.

Meanwhile, imports increased by 5.3% to JPY 10,305.8 billion, outpacing market predictions and marking the highest growth in eleven months. This increase was driven by strong domestic demand due to a major stimulus package introduced by the government.

The Nikkei 225 Index climbed 1.2% to above 53,400 while the broader Topix Index rose 0.9% to 3,622 on Thursday, snapping a five-day losing streak. The Japanese yen slipped toward 158.5 per dollar on Thursday, remaining under pressure amid a deteriorating fiscal outlook, while the Bank of Japan kicked off a two-day policy meeting.

More on Japan economy:

data.title: Microsoft wins $170.4 million U.S. Air Force cloud contract
data.description:
• Microsoft (MSFT) has been awarded a $170.4 million contract to provide cloud services to the U.S. Air Force, the U.S. Department of War said in a statement.
  • Under the deal, Microsoft will supply Azure cloud services for the Air Force’s Cloud One program, which supports a range of military users. The work will be performed at Microsoft (MSFT) facilities across the continental United States and is scheduled to run through Dec. 7, 2028.
  • The contract was awarded without competition.

data.title: Crypto custody firm BitGo raises $212.8M in U.S. IPO priced at $18 per share
data.description:
• Crypto custody firm BitGo (BTGO) priced its US IPO at $18 per share, above the originally marketed range of $15–$17, raising $212.8M.
  • The offering values the company at $2.08 billion on a fully diluted basis with Goldman Sachs (GS) and Citigroup (C) as lead underwriters.
  • For the first nine months of 2025, the company recorded net income of $8.1M on revenue of $10B, versus the previous year's net income of $5.1M on revenue of $1.9B.
  • The IPO arrives amid crypto market volatility following an October 2025 sell-off, yet benefits from President Trump's pro-crypto policies and rising institutional interest.

data.title: Aris Mining reports 22% jump in 2025 gold production, guides higher for 2026
data.description:
• Aris Mining (ARMN) on Wednesday reported full-year 2025 gold production of 256,503 ounces, up 22% from the prior year, and said output is expected to climb to 300,000–350,000 ounces in 2026, driven by the ongoing ramp-up at Segovia and the expansion of the Marmato operation.
  • The increase was driven by the expanded Segovia mill and above-guidance performance at Marmato. Fourth quarter production of 69,852 oz capped off a year of strong operational execution.
  • Aris Mining (ARMN) said H2 2025 production of 143,088 oz, up 26% over H1 2025, reflects the installation of Segovia's second ball mill in June 2025.
  • The company reported a cash balance of over $390 million at December 31, 2025, net of the $60 million cash used for the Q4 2025 acquisition of the remaining 49% interest in Soto Norte.
  • Looking ahead to 2026, the miner expects consolidated production of 300,000 to 350,000 oz, supported by continued ramp-up at Segovia and increasing production from Marmato from Q4 2026 onwards, where active mining in the wider porphyry areas is already underway, materially de-risking the project.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Kinder Morgan tops Q4 estimates; initiates FY26 outlook below forecast
ONEOK ups quarterly dividend by 4% to $1.07 per share
data.description:
• ONEOK (OKE) increased its quarterly  dividend to $1.07 per share, an increase of 4%.
  • This increase results in an annualized dividend of $4.28 per share.
  • The dividend is payable Feb. 13, 2026, to shareholders of record at the close of business Feb. 2, 2026.

data.title: Dr. Reddy's GAAP EPS of ₹14.52, revenue of ₹87.27B
data.description:
• Dr. Reddy's press release (RDY): Q3 GAAP EPS of ₹14.52.
  • Revenue of ₹87.27B (+4.4% Y/Y).

data.title: Great Southern Bancorp GAAP EPS of $1.45 beats by $0.11, revenue of $56.35M beats by $1.32M
data.description:
• Great Southern Bancorp press release (GSBC): Q4 GAAP EPS of $1.45 beats by $0.11.
  • Revenue of $56.35M (-0.2% Y/Y) beats by $1.32M.
  • For the quarter ended December 31, 2025, annualized return on average common equity was 10.16%, annualized return on average assets was 1.16%, and annualized net interest margin was 3.70%, compared to 9.76%, 1.00% and 3.49%, respectively, for the quarter ended December 31, 2024.

data.title: Winmark jumps 6% on SmallCap 600 addition, replacing Guess
data.description:
• Winmark (WINA) is set to replace Guess? (GES) in the S&P SmallCap 600 index before trading opens on January 26. 
  • This change stems from Authentic Brands Group and the Rolling Stockholders and related stockholders acquiring Guess? (GES), with the deal nearing completion pending final conditions.
  • WINA stock price climbed about 6% on Wednesday during after-market hours of trading.
